Then the Variant B view (#screen_crc_designer, Flow-Builder rail-8 pattern): editable form + preset selector (R-001/006), live KAT verdict (R-002), custom test vector (R-003), round-tripping JSON preview (R-004), Load/Save + check==compute('123456789') enforce-on-save (R-005/007), coverage strip + per-policy live preview (R-008/009), gap-conflict surfacing (R-011), preview-only guard (R-010). DEFERRED to follow-up: wiring the width-general kernel into shipped crc.py operation.

Draft-time C-35 execution probes ALL PASS vs merged keel 84180b4 (KAT 0xCBF43926; 7 presets; coverage concat=0x9C5BCBBD/fill=0x2A8A3950; gap_conflict+abort; store_word big/little; crc_lut==crc_stream). 4 keel-API discrepancies bound to real symbols: E5 loader lives in crc_designer_model.py -> crc_template.py facade; encode_word/decode_word NEW in crc.py (no big-endian decode exists); parse_job evolved-only -> flat up-convert branch + emit_job NEW; rail is fixed 9-tuple keys 1-9 -> 10th key/glyph is an open layout decision. Axis: Coverage/Certainty/Evidence all met, no unfilled template slots, no should-in-requirement. Open Qs -> Phase 2: rail key/glyph, sanitize_project_name binding, snapshot-regen closeout, RK-3.

Verified real: its PR #111 (docs-only .dev-flow/BACKLOG.md) is MERGED; origin/main advanced 84180b4->1e3125b; the session is parked (isRunning:false) waiting on a hygiene git-signal (primary-on-main). Javier is asleep (per that session). DECISION (autonomous, recorded): do NOT proceed to Phase 3 implementation and do NOT self-merge — an unattended self-merge into a two-owner conflict is the one irreversible harm and Javier's pre-collision autonomy grant cannot authorize it. Keep primary checkout on feat/batch-58-crc-designer-view (NOT main) so the other session stays parked and cannot collide. Phases 0-2 (verified requirements + cross-review) preserved as a reusable handoff for whichever session Javier picks. Await Javier's adjudication. Backstop: both flows' RC-1 already-shipped grep would catch a merged #screen_crc_designer, so whoever merges first stops the other.
